question: An ideal gas cannot be liquefied because
option1: Its critical temperature is always above \({0^o}C\)
option2: Its molecules are relatively smaller in size

option4: Forces operating between its molecules are negligible
remarks: Correct answer is "Forces operating between its molecules are negligible"
